sectionsArray.headline: Built for teams, not one-offs
sectionsArray.subheadline: Actta Studio is a production foundation for Sanity-powered marketing sites. Decisions are made once — schema factories, fetch patterns, cache tags, and agent-ready docs — so every project starts from a known baseline.

sectionsArray.headline: What we optimize for
sectionsArray.subheadline: Principles that keep content models clean and frontends fast.

features.title: Content-first routing
features.description: Pages own URIs; articles live under a fixed namespace. Editors control URLs without touching code.

features.title: Schema as system
features.description: Reusable field factories for links, media, SEO, and page builder sections — no copy-paste schema.

features.title: Production plumbing
features.description: Cache tags, webhook revalidation, draft mode, and Presentation live preview ship out of the box.

features.title: Agent-native docs
features.description: AGENTS.md and feature docs encode decisions so humans and AI assistants extend the starter consistently.

sectionsArray.headline: One app, one deploy
sectionsArray.subheadline: Embedded Studio at /studio means a single Next.js app on Vercel — no separate CMS host to configure.

sectionsArray.headline: Ready to explore?
sectionsArray.subheadline: Browse articles for setup guides, or reach out if you have questions.

seoMetadata.title: About
seoMetadata.description: About Actta Studio — a production-ready Sanity + Next.js starter.

sectionsArray.headline: Actta Studio
sectionsArray.subheadline: A production foundation for Sanity-powered marketing sites — schema factories, catch-all routing, live preview, cache tags, and embedded Studio in one Next.js app.

sectionsArray.headline: What's included
sectionsArray.subheadline: Eight pillars from schema-as-system to agent-native documentation.

features.title: Schema factories
features.description: Link, media, URI, page builder, and SEO fields — reusable across every document type.

features.title: Fetch layer
features.description: CDN bypass, Data Cache tags, and webhook revalidation on publish.

features.title: Embedded Studio
features.description: One dev server, one deploy. Studio lives at /studio on the same origin.

features.title: Live preview
features.description: Presentation Tool with in-place section updates and live site chrome.

features.title: SEO & sitemap
features.description: Per-page metadata, CMS favicon, robots.txt, and automatic sitemap generation.

features.title: Contact forms
features.description: Submissions saved to Sanity with optional Resend email notifications.

features.title: Staging gates
features.description: Site-wide Basic Auth plus per-page password protection for drafts.

features.title: Agent-ready docs
features.description: AGENTS.md and feature guides so teams extend the starter without reinventing patterns.

name: Actta Studio

footer: © Actta Studio. Built with Sanity and Next.js.

seoMetadata.title: Actta Studio
seoMetadata.description: Production-ready Sanity + Next.js starter for marketing sites.

title: Guides
description: Deployment, webhooks, and extending the starter.

title: Welcome to Actta Studio
excerpt: Everything you need to know about this starter — structure, env vars, and how to extend it.

children.text: This starter is a single Next.js app with embedded Sanity Studio. Pages use URI-based catch-all routing; articles live at /articles/[slug]. One dev server, one deploy.
Key folders
app/ — routes, API, and embedded Studio
features/ — Sanity client, page builder, site header/footer, auth
sanity/ — schema factories, Studio structure, constants
docs/ — feature documentation and setup guides
After schema changes
Run pnpm sanity:typegen to regenerate TypeScript types from your schema. Use pnpm plop to scaffold new page-builder sections.
Quick start

pnpm install
pnpm sanity:project-setup --import-seed
pnpm dev

seoMetadata.title: Welcome to Actta Studio
seoMetadata.description: Getting started with the Actta Studio Sanity + Next.js starter.

title: Mux video setup
excerpt: How to configure Mux for video uploads in Studio and render playback on the frontend.

children.text: Mux tokens are only needed in Studio for uploads — the frontend uses public playback IDs stored in Sanity documents. Hero sections and media fields support both images and Mux video.
Environment variables

SANITY_STUDIO_MUX_TOKEN_ID=your-token-id
SANITY_STUDIO_MUX_TOKEN_SECRET=your-token-secret
Using video in the page builder
Add a Hero Section to any page, set Media type to Video, and upload via Mux in Studio. Optional parallax works for both images and video — and respects prefers-reduced-motion.

seoMetadata.title: Mux video setup
seoMetadata.description: Configure Mux for Sanity Studio video uploads.

title: Deploying to Vercel
excerpt: Environment variables, build settings, and post-deploy checklist for production.

children.text: The starter is optimized for Vercel. Connect your Git repository, set environment variables, and deploy. Studio and the public site share the same deployment.
Required env vars
NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_PROJECT_ID — your Sanity project ID
NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_DATASET — usually production
NEXT_PUBLIC_URL — your production URL (no trailing slash)
Post-deploy
Add your Vercel URL to Sanity CORS origins (base URL only, with credentials). Create a webhook for cache revalidation — see the webhooks article for the projection to use.

seoMetadata.title: Deploying to Vercel
seoMetadata.description: Deploy the Actta Studio starter to Vercel.

title: Webhooks and cache revalidation
excerpt: How publish webhooks invalidate Next.js cache tags for pages, articles, and site settings.

children.text: When content is published in Sanity, a webhook POSTs to /api/revalidate. The handler invalidates cache tags for the document type, document ID, page URI, and article slug.
Webhook projection
Use this GROQ projection so the revalidate route receives uri and slug:
{ _id, _type, "uri": uri.current, "slug": slug.current }
When to set it up

Deploy first with SANITY_REVALIDATE_SECRET set, then create the webhook in manage.sanity.io. Draft mode and Presentation preview work without webhooks — webhooks are for production cache freshness after publish.

seoMetadata.title: Webhooks and cache revalidation
seoMetadata.description: Configure Sanity webhooks for Next.js cache revalidation.

title: Extending the page builder
excerpt: Add new sections with Plop — schema, React component, and registry in one command.

children.text: Pages are composed from sections in the page builder. Built-in sections include hero, features, steps, CTA, text banner, and contact form. Articles use Portable Text instead.
Scaffold a new section

pnpm plop
pnpm sanity:typegen
Naming convention
Section _type names use camelCase with a Section suffix — e.g. heroSection, featuresSection. Model what things are, not how they look, so redesigns don't break field names.

seoMetadata.title: Extending the page builder
seoMetadata.description: Add custom page-builder sections with Plop.
